result:
**Goal: Add a "long-exposure photography" mode that composites hundreds of simulation frames into a single artistic still image, revealing the full trajectory and flow patterns of any simulation — like astrophotography for cellular automata.**

This would blend frame data over a configurable time window (e.g., 50–500 generations) using accumulation buffers, producing luminance trails, flow lines, and density maps rendered as a single high-detail truecolor frame. Users could trigger a capture with a keybinding, watch it accumulate in real-time, then freeze the result as a viewable/exportable composite. It builds naturally on the existing ghost trail, heatmap, truecolor, and analytics infrastructure — but instead of fading echoes, it creates permanent, layered artwork from simulation dynamics.
